title: Aureline AI external-tool / MCP-server gateway registry: transport, execution-locus, auth mode, taint posture, allowed side-effect, data-class, approval, and invocation disclosure vocabulary
description: Boundary schema for the external_tool_registry_entry_record, external_tool_invocation_disclosure_record, and external_tool_registry_audit_event_record frozen in docs/ai/provider_model_registry_contract.md. Every external tool or MCP-server entry the product might allow an AI turn or a user-facing command to invoke — a stdio-spawned local subprocess, a loopback HTTP tool, a loopback unix-domain-socket tool, a remote HTTPS tool, a remote MCP server over stdio / local HTTP / streamable HTTP, an extension-mediated tool, or a mocked test tool — resolves to one governed row on this boundary. Two invariants are frozen on this boundary. First: external-tool output is tainted input by default. Every tool return value carries the tainted fence (schemas/ai/context_assembly.schema.json tainted_fence_strategy / tainted_usage_constraint) unless the row's tool_output_trust_posture_class is explicitly trusted_first_party_local_tool and the invocation policy admits that posture. Second: external tools MUST ride the same permission, policy, and audit model as native tools. Every admitted invocation spends an approval ticket (ADR-0010 schemas/integration/approval_ticket.schema.json) and emits on the provider_handoff and ai_context audit streams; a tool invocation that attempts to bypass the approval / policy / audit path is denied with a typed bypass-forbidden denial. Raw URLs, raw endpoint hostnames, raw spawn commands, raw environment variables, raw API keys, raw OAuth tokens, raw mTLS material, raw request / response bodies, and raw stdio frames never cross this boundary; records carry opaque refs, fingerprints, and structured fields only. The schema composes with (and does not replace) schemas/ai/provider_registry.schema.json, schemas/ai/context_assembly.schema.json (tainted fence / tainted usage vocabulary, tool_call_outcome, data-class family), schemas/providers/publish_later_record.schema.json (predicted_side_effect_class, irreversibility_class), schemas/integration/approval_ticket.schema.json (approval lineage), schemas/integration/browser_handoff_packet.schema.json (actor-class vocabulary), and schemas/extensions/*.schema.json (extension-mediated tool transport). The eventual tool-gateway crate's Rust types are the schema of record; this file is the cross-tool boundary every non-owning surface reads. Adding a new tool_class, tool_transport_class, tool_execution_locus_class, tool_auth_mode_class, tool_output_trust_posture_class, allowed_side_effect_class, allowed_data_class, approval_posture_class, denial_reason, audit_event_id, or tool_lifecycle_state_class is additive-minor and requires an external_tool_registry_schema_version bump; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

external_tool_registry_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this external-tool-registry vocabulary export. Current value is 1.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests / journal entries / support bundles / evidence packets. Raw URLs, raw endpoint hostnames, raw spawn commands, raw environment variables, raw API keys, raw OAuth tokens, raw mTLS material, raw request / response bodies, and raw stdio frames never appear here.

tool_class.description: High-level tool class. first_party_native_tool = a tool shipped with Aureline itself (e.g. a workspace-search tool hosted in-process); extension_provided_tool = a tool provided by an installed extension manifest (schemas/extensions/); mcp_server_tool = a tool exposed by an MCP server following the Model Context Protocol; user_registered_external_tool = a tool the user registered directly; enterprise_registered_external_tool = a tool an admin / policy bundle registered for managed fleets; mocked_test_tool = parity / record-replay test fixtures only; disabled_no_tool = the row exists but is suspended / withdrawn / not admitted.

tool_transport_class.description: Transport-class vocabulary for external tools. local_stdio_spawn means the tool is spawned as a subprocess on the user's device with stdio framing (typical MCP-over-stdio pattern). local_http_loopback means the tool listens on loopback HTTP. remote_https / remote_grpc_over_tls are the fully remote variants. remote_mcp_over_stdio / remote_mcp_over_local_http / remote_mcp_over_streamable_http are MCP-shaped variants (the MCP server's own transport, distinct from the AI-inference MCP variants on schemas/ai/provider_registry.schema.json). Every entry names exactly one.

tool_execution_locus_class.description: Execution-locus vocabulary naming where the tool actually runs. local_* variants never leave the device; remote_* and enterprise_gateway_brokered_service variants reach the network. Every external-tool invocation's locus is visually distinguishable from a local native-tool invocation in the invocation disclosure chip.

tool_auth_mode_class.description: Auth-mode vocabulary for the tool endpoint. byok_* = the user brought the credential and the credential is addressed through the secret broker (ADR-0007); the row MUST NOT embed the credential. signed_manifest_only = the tool is admitted only by its signed manifest (typical for first-party native tools and signed extension-provided tools).

tool_output_trust_posture_class.description: Taint posture the composer MUST apply to bytes returned by this tool when they flow back into a future AI turn. always_tainted_* = the return value is always placed under the tainted fence (schemas/ai/context_assembly.schema.json tainted_fence_strategy with the default usage-constraint set); trusted_first_party_local_tool_output is admitted only for first-party native tools whose manifest signature is verified and whose locus is local_in_process / local_subprocess_same_device; trusted_signed_extension_local_tool_output is admitted for signed extension-provided local tools. Every other posture is tainted by default.

predicted_side_effect_class.description: Side-effect class re-exported from schemas/providers/publish_later_record.schema.json without modification (ADR-0010). Every tool row lists the allowed side-effect classes the tool may predict; a tool that declares external_irreversible_publish / policy_or_trust_mutation / capability_widening / local_destructive_edit MUST carry a preview / approval posture above allowed_without_prompt.

allowed_data_class.description: Data-class allowlist vocabulary naming which ai_context_assembly source classes may be sent to the tool as an input argument. Re-exported from schemas/ai/provider_registry.schema.json without modification. credential_handle_denied_always / secret_projection_denied_always are never admitted on the allowlist; they are surfaced on the row's denied-always set so the deny record is mechanically checkable.

approval_posture_class.description: Approval-posture vocabulary the tool row advertises. Every invocation above allowed_without_prompt MUST spend an approval ticket before invocation; the tool bypass-forbidden invariant applies here identically to native-tool approvals.

tool_lifecycle_state_class.description: Lifecycle state of the tool entry. quarantined_pending_review is the canonical state for a user-registered or mirror-imported tool whose signature / manifest verification is pending or failed; a quarantined tool MUST NOT be invoked.

offline_posture_class.description: Offline-posture vocabulary re-exported from schemas/ai/provider_registry.schema.json without modification. offline_only_no_network_egress is the only admitted value when tool_execution_locus_class is one of local_in_process / local_subprocess_same_device / local_sandboxed_container_same_device / local_companion_service_loopback and tool_auth_mode_class is one of no_auth_local_spawn / no_auth_loopback_only / signed_manifest_only.

tool_output_invocation_disclosure_kind.description: Pre-invocation disclosure-kind vocabulary for external tools. The invocation disclosure record MUST surface at minimum tool_identity_chip, tool_transport_chip, tool_execution_locus_chip, tool_output_trust_posture_chip, allowed_side_effect_readout, and allowed_data_class_readout; missing kinds deny with tool_inspectability_disclosure_missing_pre_invocation.

redaction_class.description: Redaction class re-exported from schemas/governance/capability_lifecycle.schema.json without modification (ADR-0011).

signer_continuity_state_ref.description: Opaque ref to the signer-continuity-state record. Empty string when tool_class = first_party_native_tool and no external continuity chain applies.

manifest_chain_root_ref.description: Opaque ref to the root of the tool's signed manifest chain. Empty string when none applies.

tool_family_label.description: Short reviewer-visible family label. Never a raw URL or raw hostname.

tool_capability_version.description: Stable capability-version the row advertises for parity / replay correlation. Bumped when the tool's observable capability set changes.

allowed_side_effect_classes.description: Side-effect classes the tool may predict. An invocation whose predicted side effect is not in this set MUST deny with tool_side_effect_class_not_in_allowlist.

allowed_data_classes.description: Data-class allowlist naming which ai_context_assembly source classes may be sent to the tool as an input argument.

denied_always_data_classes.description: Data classes the row explicitly denies regardless of policy epoch. MUST include credential_handle_denied_always and secret_projection_denied_always so the deny record is mechanically checkable.

enterprise_gateway_broker_ref.description: Opaque ref to the enterprise-gateway broker record fronting the tool. Required (non-empty) when tool_execution_locus_class = enterprise_gateway_brokered_service.

connected_provider_record_id.description: Opaque ref to the ADR-0010 connected-provider record the tool is scoped under (BYOK tools that authenticate against a vendor the user has connected). Empty string when none applies.

extension_manifest_ref.description: Opaque ref to the extension manifest row (schemas/extensions/) that authored the tool. Required (non-empty) when tool_class = extension_provided_tool; empty string otherwise.

mcp_server_capability_ref.description: Opaque ref to the MCP-server capability descriptor (the capability block the server advertised at admission). Required (non-empty) when tool_class = mcp_server_tool; empty string otherwise.

signing_evidence_reference.description: Signing-evidence reference for the tool manifest. Required (non-null) when tool_output_trust_posture_class is trusted_first_party_local_tool_output or trusted_signed_extension_local_tool_output; null otherwise.

required_approval_ticket_ref.description: Opaque ref to the approval-ticket row that admitted the tool into the registry (first-admission approval). Required (non-empty) when approval_posture_class is above allowed_without_prompt.

quota_family_class.description: Quota-family class re-exported from schemas/ai/provider_registry.schema.json without modification.

region_posture_class.description: Region-posture class re-exported from schemas/ai/provider_registry.schema.json without modification. local_device_only is the only admitted value for tool_execution_locus_class in {local_in_process, local_subprocess_same_device, local_sandboxed_container_same_device, local_companion_service_loopback}.

retention_stance_class.description: Retention-stance class re-exported from schemas/ai/provider_registry.schema.json without modification. Describes what the tool's backend does with arguments / results after the call completes.

allOf.description: disabled_no_tool MUST carry disabled_no_transport / disabled_no_locus / disabled_no_auth.
enterprise_gateway_brokered_service MUST carry a non-empty enterprise_gateway_broker_ref.
A trusted_* tool output posture MUST carry a signing_evidence_reference and a local execution locus.
An approval posture above allowed_without_prompt MUST carry a non-empty required_approval_ticket_ref.

external_tool_registry_entry_record.description: One governed row per external-tool or MCP-server entry. Local (stdio / loopback / companion-service), remote (HTTPS / gRPC / streamable HTTP MCP), and enterprise-gateway-brokered rows are mutually distinguishable by schema alone so reviewers, admins, and support-export surfaces can see the execution locus without opening raw endpoint metadata.

data_classes_to_be_sent.description: Data classes the invocation intends to pass as tool input. Every entry MUST be in the tool row's allowed_data_classes set; a mismatch denies with tool_data_class_not_in_allowlist.

originating_approval_ticket_ref.description: Opaque ref to the approval-ticket row that admitted this invocation. External-tool invocations MUST resolve to a ticket; a ticket-missing invocation denies with tool_bypass_attempted_approval_ticket_missing.

disclosure_kinds_surfaced.description: Pre-invocation disclosure kinds the invocation surface has rendered. MUST include at minimum tool_identity_chip, tool_transport_chip, tool_execution_locus_chip, tool_output_trust_posture_chip, allowed_side_effect_readout, and allowed_data_class_readout.

invocation_origin_class.description: Origin surface that issued the invocation. support_replay_only is a reconstructed invocation for support / parity audit; it MUST NOT produce side effects.

originating_context_assembly_ref.description: Opaque ref to the ai_context_assembly_record when invocation_origin_class is one of the ai_turn_* classes. Empty string otherwise.

originating_command_invocation_ref.description: Opaque ref to the command-invocation-session record (schemas/commands/) when the invocation was routed through a user command. Empty string otherwise.

tool_call_lineage_ref.description: Opaque ref to the ai_tool_call_lineage_record (schemas/ai/context_assembly.schema.json) that will carry the tool-call outcome once the invocation completes. Empty string for pre-invocation disclosures minted before the lineage record is attached.

allOf.description: Every disclosure MUST surface the six minimum disclosure kinds.

external_tool_invocation_disclosure_reco.description: One disclosure minted at the point of intent before every external-tool invocation. Makes tool identity, transport, locus, auth mode, output trust posture, predicted side effect, admitted data classes, and approval posture inspectable so local-subprocess, loopback, remote-HTTP, and MCP tool invocations can be visually distinguished from each other and from native-tool invocations.

invocation_disclosure_ref.description: Opaque ref to the external_tool_invocation_disclosure_record when the event applies to an invocation. Empty string otherwise.

approval_ticket_ref.description: Opaque ref to the approval-ticket row that admitted the invocation. Empty string for registry-level events.

denial_reason.description: Optional denial_reason string from the denial_reason enum. Empty string for non-deny events.

external_tool_registry_audit_event_recor.description: Observable event on the ai_external_tool_registry audit stream. Never carries raw URLs, raw endpoint hostnames, raw spawn commands, raw environment variables, raw credential bodies, or raw tool response bytes.
